_about: The 'deepen' feed — distilled knowledge + tools that super-u's flywheel (kgfy + skillfy) hands to this node via POST /api/ingest-knowledge. The node RECEIVES + grounds + presents; it does not build graphs or forge skills (see docs/DEEPEN-PIPELINE.md). The entry below is a SEED (producedBy: seed-example), hand-built from the real deepseek-ai/Engram repo + paper as the worked example; real ingests merge OVER it (durable KV) and win dedup by id.

source.title: Engram: Conditional Memory via Scalable Lookup

artifacts.digest: Transformers scale COMPUTE (Mixture-of-Experts routes tokens to experts) but have no native primitive for looking knowledge UP. DeepSeek's Engram adds that missing primitive: it modernizes classic N-gram embeddings into a deterministically-addressed table with O(1) lookup — a second, complementary axis of sparsity (static memory) alongside MoE's conditional compute. The paper finds a U-shaped scaling law: for a fixed budget there's an interior optimum splitting capacity between compute and memory — going all-in on either side loses. Under iso-parameter and iso-FLOPs constraints, Engram-27B consistently beats MoE baselines on knowledge, reasoning, code, and math. Mechanistically, offloading static recall to Engram frees the early layers from pattern reconstruction, preserving effective depth for reasoning — and the huge embedding tables offload to host memory with minimal inference overhead. Why it matters for you: 'conditional memory as a sparsity axis' is a transferable design move — separate what a system should COMPUTE from what it should LOOK UP, and size each.

skills.name: Allocate the sparsity budget (compute vs memory)
skills.oneLine: Split a fixed parameter/FLOP budget between MoE compute and Engram memory at the U-curve's interior optimum.
skills.mechanism: Treat neural computation (MoE) and static memory (Engram) as two substitutable sparsity axes; the U-shaped scaling law has an interior minimum, so spend on both rather than all-in on either.
skills.characteristicMove: There is an interior optimum — adding MORE of whichever axis is currently winning LOWERS quality once you pass the knee.
goodAt: trading FLOPs for cheap host-memory lookups
notGoodAt: tiny models where the embedding table would dominate params
tasks that need dynamic composition, not static recall
useWhen: deciding how to spend a fixed parameter/compute budget across compute vs recall

skills.name: Add an O(1) memory-lookup primitive
skills.oneLine: Give a model a native 'look it up' primitive via deterministically-addressed N-gram embeddings.
skills.mechanism: Modernize N-gram embeddings into a deterministically-addressed table with O(1) lookup that offloads to host RAM with minimal inference overhead.
skills.characteristicMove: Offload the massive table to HOST memory — deterministic addressing makes the transfer cheap, so capacity stops being VRAM-bound.
goodAt: adding capacity cheaply via host offload
useWhen: a model under-recalls facts at a fixed compute budget
